2026-04-21T05:54:56.3815917Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-21T05:54:56.3816450Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-21T05:54:56.3816859Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-21T05:54:56.3817494Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-21T05:54:56.3817800Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-21T05:54:56.3818039Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-21T05:54:56.3818203Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-21T05:54:56.3818385Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-21T05:54:56.3818784Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-21T05:54:56.3819273Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-21T05:54:56.3819526Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). 2026-04-21T05:58:05.2120691Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-21T05:56:02.450327778Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58433,\"title\":\"[CostManagement] Docs: Update CHANGELOG and add README examples after TypeSpec migration\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.CostManagement`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/costmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.CostManagement/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #57745) by `@haiyuazhang`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57745 migrated `Azure.ResourceManager.CostManagement` from AutoRest/Swagger to TypeSpec generation. The migration included:\\n- Full regeneration of `Generated/` from TypeSpec spec\\n- Type renames to follow SDK naming conventions: `GenerateCostDetailsReportRequestDefinition` → `GenerateCostDetailsReportContent`, `GenerateDetailedCostReportDefinition` → `GenerateDetailedCostReportContent`, `CostAllocationRuleDefinition` → (content-equivalent)\\n- New model documentation added to `ForecastResult`, `CostManagementDimension`, `QueryResult`, `ExportRun`\\n- Bug fixes: null-guard in `ScheduledActionData` nullable struct setters, removed `EditorBrowsable(Never)` from `OperationStatusType.Complete`\\n- Custom code cleanup: deleted several manually-maintained files now handled by generator\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **CHANGELOG `1.1.0-beta.1` is empty** — the `### Features Added`, `### Breaking Changes`, `### Bugs Fixed`, and `### Other Changes` sections are all blank despite significant API changes in this migration\\n- **README has no inline code examples** — the `## Examples` section only links to `(aka.ms/redacted) without any code snippets showing common operations (creating/listing exports, querying costs, managing budgets)\\n- **No `tests/Samples/` directory exists** — there are no test-backed snippet source files for the package, meaning README placeholders cannot be populated by `Update-Snippets.ps1`\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Update CHANGELOG.md\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/costmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.CostManagement/CHANGELOG.md`\\n**Action:** Edit — fill in the `1.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section with the actual changes from PR #57745.\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 1.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- Migrated SDK generation from AutoRest to TypeSpec, enabling richer model documentation and improved alignment with the Azure REST API specification.\\n- Added documentation to `ForecastResult`, `CostManagementDimension`, `QueryResult`, and `ExportRun` models.\\n\\n### Breaking Changes\\n\\n- Renamed `GenerateCostDetailsReportRequestDefinition` to `GenerateCostDetailsReportContent` to follow Azure SDK naming conventions.\\n- Renamed `GenerateDetailedCostReportDefinition` to `GenerateDetailedCostReportContent` to follow Azure SDK naming conventions.\\n- Renamed `CostAllocationRuleDefinition` to align with the `*Content` suffix convention used for request body models.\\n- Renamed `BenefitUtilizationSummariesListInput` to `BenefitUtilizationSummariesContent`.\\n- Renamed `GrainParameter` to `GrainContent`.\\n- `ExportRunContent` replaces the prior request body model for triggering export runs.\\n\\n### Bugs Fixed\\n\\n- Fixed a null-guard bug in `ScheduledActionData` nullable struct property setters that could cause `NullReferenceException` on certain write paths.\\n- Removed incorrect `[EditorBrowsable(EditorBrowsableState.Never)]` attribute from `OperationStatusType.Complete`, making it visible to callers again.\\n\\n### Other Changes\\n\\n- Internal code generation files previously maintained by hand (`ExportDataset.cs`, `ExportProperties.cs`, `SettingResource.Serialization.cs`, `ArmCostManagementModelFactory.cs`) are now produced entirely by the TypeSpec generator and have been removed from the Custom folder.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add sample code and README snippets\\n\\nCreate a new `tests/Samples/` directory and a sample file. Use `[Test]` (NUnit) on each method. Wrap the visible code with `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion`. Wrap test-only teardown with `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif`.\\n\\n**File to create:** `sdk/costmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.CostManagement/tests/Samples/Sample1_Exports.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CostManagement;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CostManagement.Models;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.CostManagement.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_Exports\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateExport()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateCostManagementExport\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n            // Scope can be a subscription, resource group, management group, or billing account\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string scope = $\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;;\\n            string exportName = \\u0026#34;myExport\\u0026#34;;\\n\\n            var exportCollection = client.GetCostManagementExports(new Azure.Core.ResourceIdentifier(scope));\\n\\n            var exportData = new CostManagementExportData\\n            {\\n                Format = ExportFormatType.Csv,\\n                DeliveryInfo = new ExportDeliveryInfo(\\n                    new ExportDeliveryDestination(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;)\\n                    {\\n                        RootFolderPath = \\u0026#34;exports\\u0026#34;\\n                    }\\n                ),\\n                Definition = new ExportDefinition(ExportType.ActualCost, ExportTimeframeType.MonthToDate)\\n            };\\n\\n            var lro = await exportCollection.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                Azure.WaitUntil.Completed,\\n                exportName,\\n                exportData);\\n\\n            CostManagementExportResource export = lro.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created export: {export.Id}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task GetExport()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:GetCostManagementExport\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string scope = $\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;;\\n            string exportName = \\u0026#34;myExport\\u0026#34;;\\n\\n            var exportCollection = client.GetCostManagementExports(new Azure.Core.ResourceIdentifier(scope));\\n            CostManagementExportResource export = await exportCollection.GetAsync(exportName);\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Export name: {export.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Export format: {export.Data.Format}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListExports()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ListCostManagementExports\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string scope = $\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;;\\n\\n            var exportCollection = client.GetCostManagementExports(new Azure.Core.ResourceIdentifier(scope));\\n            await foreach (CostManagementExportResource export in exportCollection.GetAllAsync())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Export: {export.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Step 3: Update README.md\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/costmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.CostManagement/README.md`\\n**Action:** Replace the `## Examples` section with the following, which adds snippet placeholders that `Update-Snippets.ps1` will populate:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following section shows you several code snippets using the `Azure.ResourceManager.CostManagement` library.\\n\\n### Create a Cost Export\\n\\n````C# Snippet:CreateCostManagementExport\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### Get a Cost Export\\n\\n````\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### List Cost Exports\\n\\n````C# Snippet:ListCostManagementExports\\n```\\n```\\n\\nFor more code samples, see the [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n````\\n\\nAlso ensure the README contains all required sections. The current README uses `## Getting started` but is missing a proper `## Key concepts` section with actual content beyond a generic link. Replace the generic Key concepts section with a summary of the main resource types:\\n\\n```\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThe `Azure.ResourceManager.CostManagement` library provides access to cost and billing data through the following main resource types:\\n\\n- **`CostManagementExportResource`** – Represents a recurring or one-time cost export to a storage account. Use `GetCostManagementExports()` on a scoped resource (subscription, resource group, management group) to manage exports.\\n- **`BudgetResource`** – Represents a cost budget with alert thresholds. Use `GetBudgets()` to manage budgets on subscriptions and resource groups.\\n- **`CostManagementAlertResource`** – Represents a cost alert. Use `GetCostManagementAlerts()` to retrieve triggered alerts.\\n- **`ScheduledActionResource`** – Represents a scheduled action (e.g., scheduled email delivery of a cost view).\\n\\nAll operations are scoped to an Azure scope (subscription, resource group, management group, or billing account) expressed as an `Azure.Core.ResourceIdentifier`.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/costmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.CostManagement/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/costmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.CostManagement/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 costmanagement` — this injects the `#region Snippet:` content from the new sample file into README.md. Check `git diff` afterwards to confirm the README was populated.\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 costmanagement` — only needed if any public API changed; this migration already updated the API listing files.\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/costmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.CostManagement/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;pkgs.dev.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;releaseassets.githubusercontent.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; exportName = \\u0026#34;myExport\\u0026#34;;\\n\\n            var exportCollection = client.GetCostManagementExports(new Azure.Core.ResourceIdentifier(scope));\\n\\n            var exportData = new CostManagementExportData\\n            {\\n                Format = ExportFormatType.Csv,\\n                DeliveryInfo = new ExportDeliveryInfo(\\n                    new ExportDeliveryDestination(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;)\\n                    {\\n                        RootFolderPath = \\u0026#34;exports\\u0026#34;\\n                    }\\n                ),\\n                Definition = new ExportDefinition(ExportType.ActualCost, ExportTimeframeType.MonthToDate)\\n            };\\n\\n            var lro = await exportCollection.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                Azure.WaitUntil.Completed,\\n                exportName,\\n                exportData);\\n\\n            CostManagementExportResource export = lro.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created export: {export.Id}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task GetExport()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:GetCostManagementExport\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string scope = $\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;;\\n            string exportName = \\u0026#34;myExport\\u0026#34;;\\n\\n            var exportCollection = client.GetCostManagementExports(new Azure.Core.ResourceIdentifier(scope));\\n            CostManagementExportResource export = await exportCollection.GetAsync(exportName);\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Export name: {export.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Export format: {export.Data.Format}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListExports()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ListCostManagementExports\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string scope = $\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;;\\n\\n            var exportCollection = client.GetCostManagementExports(new Azure.Core.ResourceIdentifier(scope));\\n            await foreach (CostManagementExportResource export in exportCollection.GetAllAsync())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Export: {export.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Step 3: Update README.md\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/costmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.CostManagement/README.md`\\n**Action:** Replace the `## Examples` section with the following, which adds snippet placeholders that `Update-Snippets.ps1` will populate:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following section shows you several code snippets using the `Azure.ResourceManager.CostManagement` library.\\n\\n### Create a Cost Export\\n\\n````C# Snippet:CreateCostManagementExport\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### Get a Cost Export\\n\\n````\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### List Cost Exports\\n\\n````C# Snippet:ListCostManagementExports\\n```\\n```\\n\\nFor more code samples, see the [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n````\\n\\nAlso ensure the README contains all required sections. The current README uses `## Getting started` but is missing a proper `## Key concepts` section with actual content beyond a generic link. Replace the generic Key concepts section with a summary of the main resource types:\\n\\n```\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThe `Azure.ResourceManager.CostManagement` library provides access to cost and billing data through the following main resource types:\\n\\n- **`CostManagementExportResource`** – Represents a recurring or one-time cost export to a storage account. Use `GetCostManagementExports()` on a scoped resource (subscription, resource group, management group) to manage exports.\\n- **`BudgetResource`** – Represents a cost budget with alert thresholds. Use `GetBudgets()` to manage budgets on subscriptions and resource groups.\\n- **`CostManagementAlertResource`** – Represents a cost alert. Use `GetCostManagementAlerts()` to retrieve triggered alerts.\\n- **`ScheduledActionResource`** – Represents a scheduled action (e.g., scheduled email delivery of a cost view).\\n\\nAll operations are scoped to an Azure scope (subscription, resource group, management group, or billing account) expressed as an `Azure.Core.ResourceIdentifier`.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/costmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.CostManagement/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/costmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.CostManagement/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 costmanagement` — this injects the `#region Snippet:` content from the new sample file into README.md. Check `git diff` afterwards to confirm the README was populated.\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 costmanagement` — only needed if any public API changed; this migration already updated the API listing files.\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/costmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.CostManagement/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt;
